authoritative_sources.title: How Is Child Care Regulated to Ensure Children's Health and Safety?
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official Office of Child Care operating baseline for how licensed child-care programs are regulated and monitored, making it authoritative for identifying the governing public control model for this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: What Is Child Care Licensing?
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official guidance that names the minimum controls attached to licensed child care, including background checks, training, emergency planning, building safety, and ongoing monitoring.

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring and Inspections
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official guidance that specifies monitoring and inspection reports, substantiated complaints, and corrective-action visibility, making it directly relevant to operations exception handling and closure evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: Health and Safety Requirements
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official public control guidance for sanitation, medication handling, physicals and immunizations, building safety, and emergency planning that constrains operational progression and closure.

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Background Checks
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official control guidance naming which staff and adults require checks and the five-year renewal cadence, directly affecting whether assigned staff may work in classrooms or transportation roles.

authoritative_sources.title: Staff Qualifications and Required Training
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official guidance that ties staff roles, ages served, ongoing health and safety training topics, and transportation safety training to child-care operations readiness.

authoritative_sources.title: Report A Child Care Concern
authoritative_sources.publisher: ChildCare.gov / Administration for Children and Families
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official escalation guidance identifying state and territory channels for child-care health and safety complaints, which constrains when operations must escalate instead of closing a safety issue internally.

authoritative_sources.title: Caring for Our Children Basics
authoritative_sources.publisher: Administration for Children and Families, Office of Child Care
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Federal child-care health and safety standard used across licensing and subsidy contexts to operationalize supervision, transportation, medication, hygiene, and environmental controls.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.47 Safety practices.
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulation for child-safety systems, authorized-adult release, active supervision, medication records, hygiene, preventive maintenance, and safety-incident reporting in early-childhood operations.

authoritative_sources.title: 1302.102 Program goals, continuous improvement, and reporting.
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary management-system and reporting regulation for ongoing oversight, corrective action, and safety incident reporting, making it directly authoritative for operations rework and closure flows.

authoritative_sources.title: 1303 Subpart F—Transportation
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary transportation rule set naming vehicle operation, trip routing, safety procedures, and disability accommodations for child transport workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Active Supervision
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official workflow guidance that turns supervision requirements into concrete operational practices such as setup, position staff, scan and count, and transition control.

authoritative_sources.title: Active Supervision on Buses
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Official transportation workflow guidance that defines bus sign-in sheets, name-to-face checks, seat checks, and authorized-adult drop-off practices.

workflow_stages.name: Daily operating readiness intake
workflow_stages.description: Validate that the site, room, route, or task has the minimum staffing, qualification, training, roster, and systems context required before execution proceeds.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Classroom, site, and transport execution
workflow_stages.description: Progress room opening, attendance, supervision, release, transport, medication, hygiene, and facility controls using child-care-specific records.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, incident, and inspection handling
workflow_stages.description: Route blocked work into rework, corrective action, complaint escalation, or safety escalation when inspection, incident, or supervision evidence shows the operation cannot proceed normally.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion and closure verification
workflow_stages.description: Close the work only when attendance, release, corrective action, route closeout, or incident resolution records support completion and the systems of record agree.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Low-confidence routing and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when licensing interpretation, staffing authority, child-protection risk, clinical judgment, or jurisdiction-specific rules are needed to resolve the case safely.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: classroom ratio and staffing coverage sheet
bus roster and sign-in or sign-out sheet
staff qualification, training, and background-check record
incident report and parent notification record
medication administration record and health action plan
inspection report, substantiated complaint record, and corrective-action ticket
execution status update, exception summary, and completion record

systems_of_record.name: child-care management system
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative roster, attendance, classroom assignment, release authorization, and family contact record.

systems_of_record.name: staff credentialing and scheduling system
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for role assignment, training completion, background-check status, and classroom coverage readiness.

systems_of_record.name: transportation roster or dispatch system
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for route roster, bus sign-in or sign-out, and route completion evidence when transportation is in scope.

systems_of_record.name: incident, medication, and safety log
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for incident follow-up, MAR completion, allergy plans, and safety-sensitive exception handling.

systems_of_record.name: licensing or compliance tracker
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for inspections, complaint records, corrective actions, and unresolved compliance blockers.

systems_of_record.name: facility maintenance and work-order system
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative source for playground, building, and room readiness remediation evidence.

systems_of_record.name: document repository or e-signature store
systems_of_record.role: Authoritative archive for signed notifications, release forms, photos, repair evidence, and management verification.

allowed: Advance, hold, rework, or complete operational work based on documented readiness and evidence.
Confirm whether required operational records exist and support lifecycle progression.
Prepare evidence-backed status, completion, exception, and escalation artifacts.
escalate: Escalate when staffing allocation, licensing interpretation, child-protection risk, HR action, or legal review authority is required.
Escalate when tenant policy and jurisdiction-specific release, ratio, medication, or complaint rules materially conflict.
Escalate when cross-system child-accountability records cannot be reconciled safely.
refuse: Refuse requests to release a child without authoritative authorization evidence.
Refuse requests to close incidents, inspection items, or operational exceptions without the required evidence.
Refuse requests to override licensing, ratio, release, medication, or safety controls.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Operating with stale or missing staff-clearance records
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Child-care rooms and routes cannot safely open if assigned adults lack current background checks or required training.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Improper child release or incomplete name-to-face verification
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Release control is safety-critical and tied to explicit authorized-adult records rather than generic task completion.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Inspection or complaint work closed without corrective-action evidence
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Child-care monitoring and substantiated complaint records require visible remediation and cannot be treated as ordinary ticket closure.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Attendance, transport, and release systems disagree on child accountability
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Child-care operations must preserve a continuous chain of custody for children, making cross-system disagreement materially higher risk than a generic workflow mismatch.

domain_failure_modes.mode: Ratio or supervision conflicts handled as staffing convenience
domain_failure_modes.why_industry_specific: Classroom and bus supervision requirements are child-safety controls, not simple scheduling preferences.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific ratio, medication delegation, and complaint-reporting procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These rules vary materially by jurisdiction and tenant, so the pack records them as required runtime retrieval inputs instead of guessing.
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The public federal and official guidance baseline is sufficient to define safe refusal, escalation, and evidence posture for a validated spec pack.
